rows.criterion: single compose command brings up all four services networked together
rows.test: docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up -d --build; docker compose ps shows engine, api, agents, ui all Up (healthy)

rows.criterion: UI can successfully reach the api/ gateway
rows.test: curl http://localhost:8081/query is NOT the check (that's a GET, rewritten to index.html by design); real check is that ui's nginx proxies /query POST to api -- validated indirectly by api's own /health passing behind the same network + by ui's root page loading (nginx up, network resolves api hostname). A stronger check: curl -X POST http://localhost:8081/query with a JSON body should reach api's handler (even if it returns an error due to QueryPipeline not fully live, an HTTP response from api -- not an nginx 502 -- proves reachability).

rows.criterion: docker compose up + scripted smoke check: curl api/ health route
docker compose up + scripted smoke check: load ui/ root page
rows.test: curl -fsS http://localhost:8081/ returns 200 with body containing built index.html markers (<div id="root">)

rows.criterion: agents service does not crash-loop without real API keys (demo/local default)
rows.test: LLM_PROVIDER=ollama set via compose default; docker compose ps shows agents Up (healthy), not Restarting
deploy/.env.example contains only variable NAMES, no real key values; grep for OPENROUTER_API_KEY=/GEMINI_API_KEY= followed by a non-empty non-placeholder value returns nothing
rows.covered_by: manual review of deploy/.env.example content + git diff before commit

rows.criterion: ui Dockerfile actually builds the real Vite/React app (not a stub)
rows.test: docker compose build ui succeeds (tsc -b type-check + vite build both pass inside the builder stage)
